title: Climate Change Made Bangalore's Filter Coffee More Expensive
what_talking_about: The Bruhat Bengaluru Hotel association announced a 10-15% filter coffee price increase due to arabica powder surging Rs 150/kg between January and February.
Global coffee prices doubled over two years, linked to extreme weather — record heat and erratic rainfall slashed yields in Brazil and Vietnam.
why_care: This is a textbook example of how climate impacts on agricultural commodities show up as food inflation in your daily life.
India's Economic Survey 2024-25 confirmed food inflation reached 8.8%, among the world's highest.

sources.title: The Hindu: Bengaluru's favourite filter coffee to get costlier from next month
